conversations.say: Inez, people are asking about the ledger and the old R-associated account. We need to agree on what neighborhood details remain off-limits before outsiders start treating every informal arrangement as guilt.
conversations.offer: My support and discretion regarding neighborhood customer protections.
conversations.request: Your backing if ledger anomalies or past favors surface under scrutiny.

conversations.say: Morgan, I have transaction continuity records and collector details, but I will only discuss the pattern if you agree not to call any customer R and respect customer privacy.
conversations.offer: A bounded look at the transaction pattern and collector context.
conversations.request: A clear, binding promise on attribution limits and customer privacy.

conversations.say: Frankie, before we talk about R's habits, tell me how you remember the private routine so we don't confuse good storytelling with authentication.
conversations.offer: A comparison with the private habits noted in my counter records.
conversations.request: Your exact recollection of R's purported private habit.

public_statement: Fifteen years of keeping a shop teaches you that habits feel personal, but accounts are shared and collectors change. Before anyone buys certainty from my counter, let's be clear about what the ledger actually supports.

commitments.promise: Protect local customer records and informal arrangements from public exposure.
Restrict ledger disclosure terms and avoid naming any customer as R.

goal_progress: Contacted Inez to discuss neighborhood disclosure boundaries.
Engaged Morgan with terms for ledger discussion and attribution limits.
Prompted Frankie on R's supposed private habit.
reasoning_summary: Rafi Calderón opens Section 1 by securing neighborhood alignment with Inez, setting strict attribution terms with Morgan, and comparing notes with Frankie while maintaining the integrity of shop records against premature monetization or exposure.
